zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabetha Alicia Corbet於1430年出生在英國斯塔福爾李時，她的父親Thomas Corbet58歲，母親Jane Burley45歲。她於大約1449年與Robert Scriven在英國英格蘭藍卡爾李結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1500年在英國英格蘭史洛普爾福洛德斯利享年71歲。

nameMeanings.name: Corbet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: Walter Corbet, a royal knight in 12th-century Scotland, was the first of that name in that kingdom. His 13th-century descendants were major landholders in the Borders, and in the 14th century they acquired lands in the North of Scotland.

variants.name: Corbett
meanings: English (West Midlands, of Norman origin): nickname from Old French corbet ‘raven’, probably denoting someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

History: This is the name of a family descended from Hugh Corbet, a Norman baron who settled in Shropshire following the Norman Conquest. One of his descendants, Sir Richard Corbet, was granted land near Shrewsbury in 1223; since the 13th century, this place has been known as Moreton Corbet. The name was taken from Shropshire to Scotland in the 12th century and to northern Ireland in the 17th century, and thence to North America by at least one group of bearers of the name.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabetha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Alicia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisha
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisia
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabetha Alicia Corbet was born in 1430, in Leigh, Staffordshire, England, her father, Thomas Corbet, was 58 and her mother, Jane Burley, was 45. She married Robert Scriven about 1449, in Leigh, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. She died about 1500, in Frodesley, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabetha Alicia Corbet est née en 1430, à Leigh, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Corbet, avait 58 ans et sa mère, Jane Burley, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Scriven environ 1449, à Leigh, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1500, à Frodesley, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 71 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabetha Alicia Corbetが1430年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアレイグで生まれた時，父Thomas Corbetは58歳，母Jane Burleyは45歳であった。約1449年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアレイグでRobert Scrivenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1500年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアフロデスリーで71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabetha Alicia Corbet(이)가 1430년 영국 스태퍼드실르 리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Corbet(은)는 58세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Burley(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 대략 1449년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 리에서 Robert Scriven(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1500년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 프로데슬리에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabetha Alicia Corbet родилась в 1430 г. в местности лиг, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Corbet было 58 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Burley, 45. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Scriven около 1449 г. в местности лиг, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла около 1500 г. в местности фродесли, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год.
